title: phleum alpinum subsp. trabutii
intro: Phleum alpinum subsp. trabutii, also known as Trabut's Timothy grass, is a subspecies of the Phleum alpinum species, a type of grass native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Phleum alpinum subsp. trabutii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system. Traditionally, this plant is prepared for medicinal use through infusions, tinctures, and extracts, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and skin problems.
